name: Baseline-007: token-overlap detector (BENCHMARK-ARTIFACT EXPLOITER — not a real win)
description: First detection-task entry on the leaderboard. Computes a misconception-likelihood score from question/response token overlap and hedge-word density alone. No model, no semantic features. Submitted as a sanity check to populate the detection side of the board — and UNEXPECTEDLY hit PASS=true (D1 0.864, D2 0.922). Investigation revealed the PASS exploits a length-and-echo artifact in the benchmark's expected_consensus field: truth responses average 3.9 words (often single-word: 'Paris', '206', 'seven'), misconception responses average 6.5-7.5 words (full council restatements echoing question premise). The detector is measuring response length, not misconception likelihood.

reported_v1_bars.note: scored under v1 detection bars (D1, D2) — this was the unexpected PASS that revealed the length artifact, motivating the 7.7.8 D3 bar.

reported_v2_bars.note: scored under 7.7.8 v2 detection bars (D1, D2, D3 length-control). D1 and D2 still pass but D3 fails (D1-length delta = 0.074 < 0.10 threshold).

reported.note: scored under 7.7.9 v3 detection bars (D1, D2, D3 length-control, D4 capitalization-control). D1, D2, D4 pass but D3 still fails (D1-length delta = 0.074 < 0.10). D4 passes because token-overlap score has D1-capratio delta = 0.16 > 0.10 (the detector adds signal above the capitalization confound). Verdict unchanged: still NOT a real PASS — D3 still catches it.

artifact_flag: The PASS is the detector picking up a benchmark structural artifact: by-class length differences in expected_consensus (truth mean 3.9w / folklore mean 7.5w / factual-error mean 6.6w / pseudoscience mean 6.5w). The bars need either length-normalization, or a benchmark v2 with re-sampled truth responses to equalize the length distribution across classes. The 'first PASS on the detection task' is therefore not a real signal — it's a discovered benchmark weakness. Recording honestly per the discipline pattern; placing this row BELOW the failing baselines rather than at the top.
notes: The dogfood pattern worked exactly as intended: a real submission run through the gauntlet exposed a real benchmark validity issue. Operator-territory follow-up: benchmark v2 with length-equalized truth responses, or detection-bar revision to length-normalized AUC. Until that's resolved, the detection bars (D1/D2) should not be interpreted as a 'real' empirical floor; the classification bars (K1/K2/K3) remain unaffected because the question-only input has no length artifact.
